title: Drake Paulsen
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Drake}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Drake_Paulsen.jpg]] |name=Drake Paulsen |homeworld=[[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]{{JournalCite|2|Out of the Cradle}} |birth=[[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], SocorroBoth Drake Paulsen's birthplace of Socorro and age of 15 is provided in "[[Out of the Cradle]]" which is set in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' giving a birth year of [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]] via simple mathematics. |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Tanned |cyber= |families= |parents=*[[Kaine Paulsen]] *A [[Drake Paulsen's mother|mother]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters=Kaine Paulsen |apprentices= }} '''Drake Paulsen''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Socorran|native]] [[Homeworld/Legends|of]] the planet [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] and the son of the legendary [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Kaine Paulsen]]. Drake was himself a pirate and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], which he had been doing since his father brought him along on a smuggling run at the age of five. He was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], but untrained in any way of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. He had [[Color/Legends|brown]] hair, a deeply tanned skin, and blue eyes, and wore a large golden earring. ==Biography== ==="The Little Prince of Socorro"=== {{Quote|When did he ever see me?"''
''"When you were born, you spent the first few hours of your life in a Coynite warrior's bare hands.|Drake and Kaine Paulsen on Tra'Parr'Sratt|}} [[File:Kaine Paulsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kaine Paulsen, Drake Paulsen's father]] The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Drake Paulsen was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] to his father, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Kaine Paulsen]], and a [[Drake Paulsen's mother|mother]]. Drake spent the first few [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] of his life in the hands of Kaine's friend and [[Coynite/Legends|Coynite]] warrior [[Tra'Parr'Sratt]], who repeatedly said "Lom," the Coynite word for freedom, upon first seeing him. It was rumored that Drake's first deal was at age three with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], with whom he traded three [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] packs for a basket full of [[Rishi honeystix]]. Jabba, impressed that the kid was fluent in [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] and immensely entertained, dubbed the boy "the Little Prince of Socorro."''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'' At some point during his childhood, Drake's mother had [[Death/Legends|passed away]], and by the age of five he began [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] with his father. ===Badawzi's ask=== ====Getitng a ride==== {{Quote|Thanks for the company, kid!''"
"''Thanks for the ride!|The nomad and Drake Paulsen|}} In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Out of the Cradle]]'' to [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]]. Drake, a fifteen-year-old, got a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] ride from a [[Unidentified nomad fugitive|nomad fugitive]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The young boy, dropped off by a dune near the [[Rym Mountains|Rym mountain range]], was thanked by the nomad for his company before he stepped away from the bike and thanked the nomad for the ride. As he did so, he scurried up the dune, pulling away a sand-saturated rag from his face and getting refreshed by Socorro's cool [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]]. The nomad then told Drake about approaching bounty hunters before he left into the flatlands. The boy continued to make his way up the dune, exhaustedly throwing himself against before turning to catch a glimpse of the nomad's leaving shadow. A sighing Drake leaned over the dune and overheard the whine of the approaching hunters speeder bikes. The boy confidently waved at the bounty hunters and smiled when they paused at him. Drake momentarily thought the hunters might come after him and instinctively thumbed the restraint from his sporting blaster, though, to the hunters, there was nothing to be gained from the boy. When the hunters moved onto the [[Doaba Badlands]], Drake sighed and pushed his [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]] back against his [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and absently messed with his earring as his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] struggled against Socorro's [[Star/Legends|sun]]. He then tugged the leather cord around his neck and pulled himself up from the sand, stiffening himself up and pulling himself up to the top of a ridge. ====Into the spaceport==== {{Quote|Always remember where you came from, boy. It's one thing nobody can take from you.|Toob Ancher to Drake Paulsen|}} [[File:Toob Ancher AJ5.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Paulsen struggled to look at Toob Ancher (pictured).]] The Socorran came across [[Soco-Jarel Spaceport]], and underneath himself he could he the various goings-on in the port, albeit muffled. Drake continued to carefully navigate the unsteady ridge and paused while he did to thrust his hands into his pockets and catch a final glimpse at the moving storm clouds. [[Toob Ancher]], an associate of Drake's father, called out for Kaine, pulling him out of reverie. The boy continued down the ridge and slid down the final slope, keeping the sun just over his right shoulder, an old superstition for good luck. Drake replied to the man that it was just him, and Ancher told the Socorran that he heard rumors of his father coming back, and that it seemed that the boy was running light on arms. Looking at Ancher's face, damaged by an explosion, Drake was horrified, though he disguised it with embarrassment. The Socorran mentally recalled the explosion, which left a would-be bounty hunter and six others dead. Drake then confessed that his father did not think he was not ready for a heavy blaster while staring away from Ancher's face. The man told the boy that the galaxy was no-longer a safe place and continued with a saying that Drake finished for him. Ancher then noticed the Socorran's bag of childhood mementos and smiled looking at them. The man continued that was going away to Vedis IV and Drake to look for him if he and his father ever needed a place. After telling the man he'd tell his father, Ancher told him to remember where he came from, as it was the one thing that could not be taken from him. As Ancher brushed past the boy into the spaceport's docking bay he watched the man's back and understood that he was going offworld to die. Feeling pity, the boy watched Ancher's ship, the ''[[Glory (YT-1300 light freighter)|Glory]]'', leave Socorro's atmosphere while he untangled his earring from his hair. Drake then silently moved into the spaceport to go to [[Black Dust Tavern]]. ====Taggert's capture==== [[File:Drake.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Drake Paulsen with Taggert and Coynite Tra'Parr'Sratt.]] Shortly after, the Socorran [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] crimelord [[Abdi-Badawzi]] forced Drake into accompanying the alcoholic [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler [[Elias Halbert]] and Tra'Pra'Sratt on a gunrun aboard the ''[[Seldom Different]]''. They were intercepted by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] customs force led by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Taggert]]. While managing to hide the cargo, Drake was fired upon and injured by one of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] that made up the boarding party, as he tried to prevent Taggert from executing Sratt, whom Taggert was intent on making an example out of. As the ''Seldom Different'' checked out clear, Taggert decided to let the Corellian, his ship, and his passengers go. This was Drake's first encounter with the Empire. Sratt nursed Drake until they made it back to Socorro, and Drake could finally be reunited with his father. ==Behind the scenes== Drake Paulsen first appeared in ''[[Out of the Cradle]]''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Drake Paulsen}} *{{JournalCite|2|Out of the Cradle|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *{{JournalCite|3|When the Domino Falls|reprint=1}} *{{JournalCite|5|A Bitter Winter|reprint=1}} *{{JournalCite|12|Idol Intentions}} ==Sources== *''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Paulsen, Drake}} [[Category:Force-sensitives]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pirates]] [[Category:Smugglers]] [[Category:Socorrans]]
